title: HCI shared-storage pool capacity and growth-rate ETA-to-full
exclusions: Not UC-19.2.27 cluster-aggregate vSAN only without a pool name join. Not in-guest disk free space, not traditional SAN or NAS-only arrays without a matching sourcetype, not object-store bucket quotas, and not the Proxmox, Cinder, or oVirt pool UCs (UC-19.2.33–.36) which you still deploy for those products. This UC is not a replacement for vSAN per-disk-group health or Nutanix curator nuance: it is cross-vendor pool headroom, growth, and ETA in one pass.
evidence: Scheduled saved search on `index=hci` (or the index union) at 10–15m cadence, 13+ month online retention, version-controlled `lookups/hci_pool_owner.csv` and `lookups/hci_pool_growth_baseline.csv` in the app, plus alert or summary-index records that retain `cluster_name`, `pool_name`, `vendor`, `pool_used_pct`, `eta_to_full_days`, `daily_growth_bytes`, `daily_growth_bytes_baseline`, `growth_burst_factor`, and `owner_email` for audit replay of each triggered row.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a test index, send synthetic HEC for `cisco:hyperflex:datastore` with `usedCapacityInBytes` increasing each poll for 6h, set `hci_pool_owner` to low `used_critical_pct` for that `cluster_name` and `dsName` as `pool_name`, and confirm the saved search shows rising `daily_growth_bytes` and a finite `eta_to_full_days` when the delta is non-zero, with `severity_bucket` crossing into high or critical on threshold. Add a second vendor sourcetype to confirm two `vendor` values in the same result.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Set `change_freeze_until` to a date two weeks in the future, reload the lookup, and confirm a row that would page as `high` is now `low` and `freeze_active=1` while the numeric columns are unchanged. Remove the freeze and see the original `severity_bucket` return.

dataSources: PRIMARY: custom HEC `vmware:vsan:capacity` from vSphere REST `GET https://<vcenter>/api/vcenter/vsan/cluster/{cluster}/capacity` with `total_capacity_b`, `used_capacity_b`, `actual_used_capacity_b`, `slack_space_b`, and efficiency fields; optional `vmware:vsan:storage_policy_compliance` and optional `vmware:vsphere:datastore` (Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere, Splunkbase 5380). Nutanix: `nutanix:storage_container` and `nutanix:storage_pool` from Splunk_TA_nutanix (5076) with `name`, `cluster_name`, `max_capacity`, `usage`, `effective_used_size_bytes`, `total_explicit_reserved_capacity_bytes`. HyperFlex: HEC `cisco:hyperflex:datastore` from `GET /rest/datastores` with `dsName`, `clusterName`, `usedCapacityInBytes`, `freeCapacityInBytes`, `provisionedCapacityInBytes`, `dataReductionFactor`. Azure Stack HCI: HEC `azure_stack_hci:volume` with `friendly_name`, `cluster_name`, `size`, `size_remaining`, `health_status`, `operational_status`. Indexes: `hci` unified, or `vmware`, `nutanix`, `cisco`, `microsoft` with OR in each arm as implemented.
app: Splunk_TA_nutanix (5076) for Nutanix. Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere (5380) optional for VMFS. Customer app `TA_hci_pool_capacity` (or equivalent) packages HEC pollers for `vmware:vsan:capacity`, `cisco:hyperflex:datastore`, `azure_stack_hci:volume`, the two lookup CSVs, and `transforms.conf`. Optional Splunk ITSI for pool entities and KPIs.
spl:
`comment("UC-19.2.2 — HCI shared-storage pool capacity, growth rate, ETA-to-full across vSAN/Nutanix/HyperFlex/Azure Stack HCI. SOURCES: vmware:vsan:capacity, nutanix:storage_container, cisco:hyperflex:datastore, azure_stack_hci:volume; LOOKUPS: hci_pool_owner, hci_pool_growth_baseline; FREEZE: change_freeze_until.")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=hci OR index=vmware) sourcetype="vmware:vsan:capacity" earliest=-24h@h
      | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, cluster, cluster_id, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
      | eval pool_name=toString(coalesce(vsan_pool_label, cluster_name + "-vSAN-pool", "vSAN-pool"))
      | eval pool_size_bytes=tonumber(toString(coalesce(total_capacity_b, totalCapacityB, total_bytes, "0")), 10)
      | eval pool_used_bytes=tonumber(toString(coalesce(used_capacity_b, usedCapacityB, used_bytes, actual_used_capacity_b, actualUsedCapacityB, "0")), 10)
      | eval vendor="vsan" ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=nutanix) sourcetype="nutanix:storage_container" earliest=-24h@h
      | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
      | eval pool_name=toString(coalesce(name, container_name, "nutanix-container"))
      | eval pool_size_bytes=tonumber(toString(coalesce(max_capacity, maxCapacity, total_capacity, "0")), 10)
      | eval pool_used_bytes=tonumber(toString(coalesce(usage, used_bytes, usedCapacityBytes, effective_used_size_bytes, "0")), 10)
      | eval vendor="nutanix" ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=cisco) sourcetype="cisco:hyperflex:datastore" earliest=-24h@h
      | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(clusterName, cluster_name, cluster, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
      | eval pool_name=toString(coalesce(dsName, datastoreName, name, "hx-ds"))
      | eval uhx=tonumber(toString(coalesce(usedCapacityInBytes, "0")), 10)
      | eval fhx=tonumber(toString(coalesce(freeCapacityInBytes, "0")), 10)
      | eval pool_size_bytes=if((uhx + fhx) > 0, uhx + fhx, tonumber(toString(coalesce(provisionedCapacityInBytes, size_bytes, total_bytes, "0")), 10))
      | eval pool_used_bytes=uhx
      | eval vendor="hyperflex" ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=microsoft) sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:volume" earliest=-24h@h
      | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, hci_cluster, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
      | eval pool_name=toString(coalesce(friendly_name, FriendlyName, name, "csv-vol"))
      | eval pool_size_bytes=tonumber(toString(coalesce(size, Size, total_bytes, "0")), 10)
      | eval srem=tonumber(toString(coalesce(size_remaining, SizeRemaining, free_bytes, "0")), 10)
      | eval pool_used_bytes=if((isnotnull(pool_size_bytes)) AND (isnotnull(srem)) AND (pool_size_bytes >= srem), pool_size_bytes - srem, tonumber(toString(coalesce(used_bytes, "0")), 10))
      | eval vendor="azure_stack_hci" ]
| where (isnotnull(cluster_name)) AND (isnotnull(pool_name)) AND (isnotnull(vendor)) AND (isnotnull(pool_size_bytes)) AND (pool_size_bytes > 0) AND (isnotnull(pool_used_bytes)) AND (pool_used_bytes >= 0) AND (pool_used_bytes <= pool_size_bytes * 1.0001)
| sort 0 +_time, cluster_name, pool_name, vendor
| streamstats global=f window=288 current=t first(pool_used_bytes) AS used_24h_ago BY cluster_name, pool_name, vendor
| eval daily_growth_bytes=if((isnotnull(used_24h_ago)) AND (isnotnull(pool_used_bytes)) AND (pool_used_bytes >= used_24h_ago), pool_used_bytes - used_24h_ago, 0)
| streamstats global=f window=2016 current=f first(pool_used_bytes) AS used_7d_ago BY cluster_name, pool_name, vendor
| eval dgb_7d_roll=if((isnotnull(used_7d_ago)) AND (isnotnull(pool_used_bytes)) AND (pool_used_bytes >= used_7d_ago), (pool_used_bytes - used_7d_ago) / 7.0, 0)
| eval dgb_7d_roll=if((dgb_7d_roll < 0), 0, dgb_7d_roll)
| stats max(_time) AS _time
    latest(cluster_name) AS cluster_name
    latest(pool_name) AS pool_name
    latest(vendor) AS vendor
    latest(pool_size_bytes) AS pool_size_bytes
    latest(pool_used_bytes) AS pool_used_bytes
    latest(daily_growth_bytes) AS daily_growth_bytes
    latest(dgb_7d_roll) AS dgb_7d_roll
  BY cluster_name, pool_name, vendor
| eval pool_used_pct=round(100.0 * pool_used_bytes / pool_size_bytes, 2)
| join type=left max=0 cluster_name, pool_name
    [| inputlookup hci_pool_owner
     | eval l_vendor=toString(vendor)
     | eval l_business=toString(business_service)
     | eval l_tier=toString(tier)
     | eval l_ucrit=tonumber(toString(coalesce(used_critical_pct, "90")), 10)
     | eval l_uhi=tonumber(toString(coalesce(used_high_pct, "80")), 10)
     | eval l_ecrit=tonumber(toString(coalesce(eta_critical_days, "7")), 10)
     | eval l_ehi=tonumber(toString(coalesce(eta_high_days, "14")), 10)
     | eval l_email=toString(owner_email)
     | eval l_freeze=toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, ""))
     | table cluster_name, pool_name, l_vendor, l_business, l_tier, l_ucrit, l_uhi, l_ecrit, l_ehi, l_email, l_freeze ]
| join type=left max=0 cluster_name, pool_name
    [| inputlookup hci_pool_growth_baseline
     | eval dgb_csv=tonumber(toString(coalesce(daily_growth_bytes_baseline, "0")), 10)
     | table cluster_name, pool_name, dgb_csv ]
| eval dgb_eff=if((isnotnull(dgb_csv)) AND (dgb_csv > 0), dgb_csv, if((dgb_7d_roll > 0), dgb_7d_roll, null()))
| eval daily_growth_bytes_baseline=if((isnotnull(dgb_eff)) AND (dgb_eff > 0), dgb_eff, if((dgb_7d_roll > 0), dgb_7d_roll, 1))
| eval growth_burst_factor=round(daily_growth_bytes / max(daily_growth_bytes_baseline, 1), 2)
| eval eta_to_full_days=if((isnotnull(daily_growth_bytes)) AND (daily_growth_bytes > 0) AND ((pool_size_bytes - pool_used_bytes) > 0), (pool_size_bytes - pool_used_bytes) / max(daily_growth_bytes, 1), null())
| eval u_crit=tonumber(toString(coalesce(l_ucrit, "90")), 10)
| eval u_high=tonumber(toString(coalesce(l_uhi, "80")), 10)
| eval e_crit=tonumber(toString(coalesce(l_ecrit, "7")), 10)
| eval e_high=tonumber(toString(coalesce(l_ehi, "14")), 10)
| eval sev0=case(
     ((isnotnull(pool_used_pct)) AND (pool_used_pct >= u_crit)) OR ((isnotnull(eta_to_full_days)) AND (eta_to_full_days <= e_crit)), "critical",
     ((isnotnull(pool_used_pct)) AND (pool_used_pct >= u_high)) OR ((isnotnull(eta_to_full_days)) AND (eta_to_full_days <= e_high)), "high",
     ((isnotnull(dgb_eff)) AND (daily_growth_bytes > 3 * dgb_eff) AND (pool_used_pct > 50)) OR ((isnull(dgb_csv)) AND (dgb_7d_roll > 0) AND (daily_growth_bytes > 3 * dgb_7d_roll) AND (pool_used_pct > 50)), "medium",
     (pool_used_pct >= 60), "low",
     1=1, "ok" )
| eval ft=coalesce(strptime(l_freeze, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%Z"), strptime(l_freeze, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), strptime(l_freeze, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), strptime(l_freeze, "%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval freeze_active=if((isnotnull(l_freeze)) AND (len(l_freeze) > 4) AND (isnotnull(ft)) AND (now() < ft), 1, 0)
| eval severity_bucket=if((freeze_active=1) AND (sev0="critical"), "medium", if((freeze_active=1) AND (sev0="high"), "low", sev0))
| eval business_service=toString(l_business)
| eval tier=toString(l_tier)
| eval owner_email=toString(l_email)
| table cluster_name, pool_name, vendor, pool_size_bytes, pool_used_bytes, pool_used_pct, daily_growth_bytes, daily_growth_bytes_baseline, growth_burst_factor, eta_to_full_days, severity_bucket, freeze_active, business_service, tier, owner_email
description: Monitors pool-level and datastore- or container- or CSV-level used space and trailing-window growth for vSAN, Nutanix, HyperFlex, and Azure Stack HCI; joins two CSVs on `cluster_name` and `pool_name`; projects `eta_to_full_days` from byte growth; classifies with a five-bucket `severity_bucket` and change-freeze demotion. Complements UC-19.2.27 (cluster-aggregate vSAN) and UC-19.2.33–.36 (other pool surfaces); this is the four-vendor unified pool view above the vendor-specific UCs. No CIM datamodel; raw sourcetypes only.
value: Shared storage exhaustion stops writes and snapshots the same way on every stack; cross-vendor normalization and one `severity_bucket` give one on-call and one business conversation when the estate mixes vSAN, Nutanix, HyperFlex, and Azure Stack HCI. A trailing growth number plus a governance CSV and freeze fields turn a capacity metric into a workflow with owners and a realistic linear ETA where growth exists.
implementation: Delivers a single cross-vendor pool health score, tier and freeze aware, with a ticket payload that already contains `owner_email` and the pool identifiers your CMDB and ITSI expect, using cross-vendor normalization on four HCI stacks. Pair with the sibling UCs for vendor-only remediation when this row turns red.
visualization: Gauges of `pool_used_pct` by `vendor`, a `timechart` of finite `eta_to_full_days`, a pool-by-day heat of `daily_growth_bytes` from a summary or the alert output, a top-10 table on smallest positive ETA, a freeze-active banner, and a wide table matching the `table` clause. Use drilldowns on `cluster_name`, `pool_name`, and `vendor` to vendor consoles. Optional deep panels for Nutanix erasure-coding and HyperFlex data reduction without mixing into the main multisearch.

references.title: Splunk — Use the HTTP Event Collector

knownFalsePositives: HyperFlex thin `provisionedCapacityInBytes` can outpace `used+free` while orchestration pre-reserves; burst factor can wobble until the project lands. Nutanix dedupe and EC change logical to physical, so a flat `pool_used_pct` can still be risky if physical rebuild margin is low. vSAN `slack_space_b` and policy reserve slices differ from some Prism or Azure charts; a green vendor UI with a high `pool_used_pct` here usually means a definition mismatch, not a Splunk failure. Azure Stack HCI Storage Bus Cache and Storage Replica maintenance can free or consume CSV space with little VM IO, swinging ETA for an hour. A single-day clone job can make `eta_to_full_days` look short for a week; compare the CSV weekly baseline. These patterns are not the vSAN health-test and stretch slack story from UC-19.2.27; they are pool-name and multi-vendor accounting effects.

premiumApps.name: Splunk ITSI
premiumApps.note: optional — one entity per pool, KPIs on pool_used_pct and eta_to_full_days

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites

This is the vendor-agnostic pool view: cross-vendor normalization maps vSAN, Nutanix, HyperFlex, and Azure Stack HCI pool or datastore or container or CSV events into one row shape so a mixed estate gets one scorecard. UC-19.2.27 remains the vSAN cluster-aggregate and slack focus on the same REST family when you only need cluster roll-up. UC-19.2.33 (Proxmox Ceph), UC-19.2.34 (LVM-thin), UC-19.2.35 (Cinder), and UC-19.2.36 (oVirt) are the other pool-level surfaces for those stacks; this UC is the four-vendor multi-HCI unification you run when those are out of scope. `vmware:vsan:capacity`, `cisco:hyperflex:datastore`, and `azure_stack_hci:volume` require a customer-built HEC poller. `nutanix:storage_container` is supplied by Splunk_TA_nutanix (Splunkbase 5076). You must run at least one poller; missing sourcetypes simply yield no events.

Create `lookups/hci_pool_owner.csv` with: `cluster_name`, `pool_name`, `vendor` (`vsan|nutanix|hyperflex|azure_stack_hci`), `business_service`, `tier` (`tier-1|tier-2|tier-3`), `used_critical_pct` (default 90), `used_high_pct` (80), `eta_critical_days` (7), `eta_high_days` (14), `owner_email`, `change_freeze_until`. Create `lookups/hci_pool_growth_baseline.csv` with: `cluster_name`, `pool_name`, `daily_growth_bytes_baseline`, `weekly_growth_bytes_baseline`, `last_updated_epoch`. The join keys are `cluster_name` plus `pool_name`; the vSAN pool name in the SPL defaults to the cluster name plus the suffix `-vSAN-pool` so owner rows stay stable across API versions.

Step 1 — Configure data collection

vSAN: HEC from `GET https://<vcenter_fqdn>/api/vcenter/vsan/cluster/{cluster}/capacity` into `sourcetype=vmware:vsan:capacity` with `total_capacity_b`, `used_capacity_b`, `actual_used_capacity_b`, optional `slack_space_b`, and efficiency fields. Optional `vmware:vsan:storage_policy_compliance` if you need SPBM-reserved headroom. Optional `vmware:vsphere:datastore` (Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere, Splunkbase 5380) for VMFS on the same app tier if you have mixed pools.

Nutanix: install Splunk_TA_nutanix (5076), enable inputs that post `nutanix:storage_container` (and `nutanix:storage_pool` for physical underlay). Use `index=nutanix` or `index=hci`.

Cisco HyperFlex: poll HX Connect `GET /rest/datastores` and map JSON to `cisco:hyperflex:datastore` with `dsName`, `clusterName`, `usedCapacityInBytes`, `freeCapacityInBytes`, `provisionedCapacityInBytes`, and `dataReductionFactor`.

Azure Stack HCI: run PowerShell `Get-Volume` (or a composed CSV inventory) to JSON, one line per object, to `sourcetype=azure_stack_hci:volume` with `friendly_name`, `cluster_name`, `size`, `size_remaining`, `health_status`, `operational_status`.

`inputs.conf` (example) on a poller host:
```ini
[script://$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/TA_hci_pool_cap/local/vsan_pool_hec.sh]
python.version = python3
interval = 300
sourcetype = vmware:vsan:capacity
index = hci
disabled = 0
```

`transforms.conf`:
```ini
[hci_pool_owner]
filename = hci_pool_owner.csv
min_matches = 0
[hci_pool_growth_baseline]
filename = hci_pool_growth_baseline.csv
min_matches = 0
```

`props.conf` (each sourcetype): `TRUNCATE=0`, `SHOULD_LINEMERGE=false`, `INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS=json` for JSON/HEC, plus `TIME_PREFIX` to your `poll_utc` field when you add it.

Step 2 — Create search and alert

The saved search runs the `spl` field. The first command is a `comment` so logs show UC-19.2.2. A `multisearch` with four `search` arms joins vSAN, Nutanix, HyperFlex, and Azure; each subsearch has `earliest=-24h@h` on its base. The pipeline uses `coalesce()` so camelCase and snake_case keys both resolve. vSAN is one pool per vSAN cluster in this model; the pool label is `cluster_name` plus the suffix to match the lookup. HyperFlex `pool_size_bytes` uses `usedCapacityInBytes` plus `freeCapacityInBytes` when both are present, else provisioned size; `pool_used_bytes` is the used counter. Azure uses `size` and `size_remaining` for used bytes.

`sort` and `streamstats` with `window=288` and `by cluster_name, pool_name, vendor` set up a 24h trailing window at five-minute sampling; change 288/2016 if your poller is not five minutes. `first(pool_used_bytes)` in that window is subtracted from the current used count to get `daily_growth_bytes` as a byte delta proxy. A second `streamstats` with `window=2016` seeds `dgb_7d_roll` for a per-day number when the baseline CSV is empty.

`stats` collapses the stream to the latest state per key. Two `inputlookup` joins pull tier thresholds, ownership, and optional `dgb_csv`. `dgb_eff` prefers the CSV; otherwise the rolling value. The `case` statement assigns `critical` and `high` from `used_*` and `eta_*` gates and finite `eta_to_full_days`. The `medium` line triggers when growth exceeds three times the effective baseline and used percent is above fifty. The change-freeze path demotes `critical` to `medium` and `high` to `low` for paging.

`savedsearches.conf` (fragment):
```ini
[HCI pool ETA critical and high]
dispatch.earliest_time = -24h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
cron_schedule = */10 * * * *
alert.suppress = 1
alert.suppress.fields = cluster_name, pool_name, vendor, severity_bucket
alert.suppress.period = 30m
search = | savedsearch ss_uc_19_2_2_hci_pool_cap_eta | where (severity_bucket="critical") OR (severity_bucket="high")
```

Fenced SPL (must match the `spl` field):

```spl
`comment("UC-19.2.2 — HCI shared-storage pool capacity, growth rate, ETA-to-full across vSAN/Nutanix/HyperFlex/Azure Stack HCI. SOURCES: vmware:vsan:capacity, nutanix:storage_container, cisco:hyperflex:datastore, azure_stack_hci:volume; LOOKUPS: hci_pool_owner, hci_pool_growth_baseline; FREEZE: change_freeze_until.")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=hci OR index=vmware) sourcetype="vmware:vsan:capacity" earliest=-24h@h
      | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, cluster, cluster_id, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
      | eval pool_name=toString(coalesce(vsan_pool_label, cluster_name + "-vSAN-pool", "vSAN-pool"))
      | eval pool_size_bytes=tonumber(toString(coalesce(total_capacity_b, totalCapacityB, total_bytes, "0")), 10)
      | eval pool_used_bytes=tonumber(toString(coalesce(used_capacity_b, usedCapacityB, used_bytes, actual_used_capacity_b, actualUsedCapacityB, "0")), 10)
      | eval vendor="vsan" ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=nutanix) sourcetype="nutanix:storage_container" earliest=-24h@h
      | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
      | eval pool_name=toString(coalesce(name, container_name, "nutanix-container"))
      | eval pool_size_bytes=tonumber(toString(coalesce(max_capacity, maxCapacity, total_capacity, "0")), 10)
      | eval pool_used_bytes=tonumber(toString(coalesce(usage, used_bytes, usedCapacityBytes, effective_used_size_bytes, "0")), 10)
      | eval vendor="nutanix" ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=cisco) sourcetype="cisco:hyperflex:datastore" earliest=-24h@h
      | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(clusterName, cluster_name, cluster, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
      | eval pool_name=toString(coalesce(dsName, datastoreName, name, "hx-ds"))
      | eval uhx=tonumber(toString(coalesce(usedCapacityInBytes, "0")), 10)
      | eval fhx=tonumber(toString(coalesce(freeCapacityInBytes, "0")), 10)
      | eval pool_size_bytes=if((uhx + fhx) > 0, uhx + fhx, tonumber(toString(coalesce(provisionedCapacityInBytes, size_bytes, total_bytes, "0")), 10))
      | eval pool_used_bytes=uhx
      | eval vendor="hyperflex" ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=microsoft) sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:volume" earliest=-24h@h
      | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, hci_cluster, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
      | eval pool_name=toString(coalesce(friendly_name, FriendlyName, name, "csv-vol"))
      | eval pool_size_bytes=tonumber(toString(coalesce(size, Size, total_bytes, "0")), 10)
      | eval srem=tonumber(toString(coalesce(size_remaining, SizeRemaining, free_bytes, "0")), 10)
      | eval pool_used_bytes=if((isnotnull(pool_size_bytes)) AND (isnotnull(srem)) AND (pool_size_bytes >= srem), pool_size_bytes - srem, tonumber(toString(coalesce(used_bytes, "0")), 10))
      | eval vendor="azure_stack_hci" ]
| where (isnotnull(cluster_name)) AND (isnotnull(pool_name)) AND (isnotnull(vendor)) AND (isnotnull(pool_size_bytes)) AND (pool_size_bytes > 0) AND (isnotnull(pool_used_bytes)) AND (pool_used_bytes >= 0) AND (pool_used_bytes <= pool_size_bytes * 1.0001)
| sort 0 +_time, cluster_name, pool_name, vendor
| streamstats global=f window=288 current=t first(pool_used_bytes) AS used_24h_ago BY cluster_name, pool_name, vendor
| eval daily_growth_bytes=if((isnotnull(used_24h_ago)) AND (isnotnull(pool_used_bytes)) AND (pool_used_bytes >= used_24h_ago), pool_used_bytes - used_24h_ago, 0)
| streamstats global=f window=2016 current=f first(pool_used_bytes) AS used_7d_ago BY cluster_name, pool_name, vendor
| eval dgb_7d_roll=if((isnotnull(used_7d_ago)) AND (isnotnull(pool_used_bytes)) AND (pool_used_bytes >= used_7d_ago), (pool_used_bytes - used_7d_ago) / 7.0, 0)
| eval dgb_7d_roll=if((dgb_7d_roll < 0), 0, dgb_7d_roll)
| stats max(_time) AS _time
    latest(cluster_name) AS cluster_name
    latest(pool_name) AS pool_name
    latest(vendor) AS vendor
    latest(pool_size_bytes) AS pool_size_bytes
    latest(pool_used_bytes) AS pool_used_bytes
    latest(daily_growth_bytes) AS daily_growth_bytes
    latest(dgb_7d_roll) AS dgb_7d_roll
  BY cluster_name, pool_name, vendor
| eval pool_used_pct=round(100.0 * pool_used_bytes / pool_size_bytes, 2)
| join type=left max=0 cluster_name, pool_name
    [| inputlookup hci_pool_owner
     | eval l_vendor=toString(vendor)
     | eval l_business=toString(business_service)
     | eval l_tier=toString(tier)
     | eval l_ucrit=tonumber(toString(coalesce(used_critical_pct, "90")), 10)
     | eval l_uhi=tonumber(toString(coalesce(used_high_pct, "80")), 10)
     | eval l_ecrit=tonumber(toString(coalesce(eta_critical_days, "7")), 10)
     | eval l_ehi=tonumber(toString(coalesce(eta_high_days, "14")), 10)
     | eval l_email=toString(owner_email)
     | eval l_freeze=toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, ""))
     | table cluster_name, pool_name, l_vendor, l_business, l_tier, l_ucrit, l_uhi, l_ecrit, l_ehi, l_email, l_freeze ]
| join type=left max=0 cluster_name, pool_name
    [| inputlookup hci_pool_growth_baseline
     | eval dgb_csv=tonumber(toString(coalesce(daily_growth_bytes_baseline, "0")), 10)
     | table cluster_name, pool_name, dgb_csv ]
| eval dgb_eff=if((isnotnull(dgb_csv)) AND (dgb_csv > 0), dgb_csv, if((dgb_7d_roll > 0), dgb_7d_roll, null()))
| eval daily_growth_bytes_baseline=if((isnotnull(dgb_eff)) AND (dgb_eff > 0), dgb_eff, if((dgb_7d_roll > 0), dgb_7d_roll, 1))
| eval growth_burst_factor=round(daily_growth_bytes / max(daily_growth_bytes_baseline, 1), 2)
| eval eta_to_full_days=if((isnotnull(daily_growth_bytes)) AND (daily_growth_bytes > 0) AND ((pool_size_bytes - pool_used_bytes) > 0), (pool_size_bytes - pool_used_bytes) / max(daily_growth_bytes, 1), null())
| eval u_crit=tonumber(toString(coalesce(l_ucrit, "90")), 10)
| eval u_high=tonumber(toString(coalesce(l_uhi, "80")), 10)
| eval e_crit=tonumber(toString(coalesce(l_ecrit, "7")), 10)
| eval e_high=tonumber(toString(coalesce(l_ehi, "14")), 10)
| eval sev0=case(
     ((isnotnull(pool_used_pct)) AND (pool_used_pct >= u_crit)) OR ((isnotnull(eta_to_full_days)) AND (eta_to_full_days <= e_crit)), "critical",
     ((isnotnull(pool_used_pct)) AND (pool_used_pct >= u_high)) OR ((isnotnull(eta_to_full_days)) AND (eta_to_full_days <= e_high)), "high",
     ((isnotnull(dgb_eff)) AND (daily_growth_bytes > 3 * dgb_eff) AND (pool_used_pct > 50)) OR ((isnull(dgb_csv)) AND (dgb_7d_roll > 0) AND (daily_growth_bytes > 3 * dgb_7d_roll) AND (pool_used_pct > 50)), "medium",
     (pool_used_pct >= 60), "low",
     1=1, "ok" )
| eval ft=coalesce(strptime(l_freeze, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%Z"), strptime(l_freeze, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), strptime(l_freeze, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), strptime(l_freeze, "%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval freeze_active=if((isnotnull(l_freeze)) AND (len(l_freeze) > 4) AND (isnotnull(ft)) AND (now() < ft), 1, 0)
| eval severity_bucket=if((freeze_active=1) AND (sev0="critical"), "medium", if((freeze_active=1) AND (sev0="high"), "low", sev0))
| eval business_service=toString(l_business)
| eval tier=toString(l_tier)
| eval owner_email=toString(l_email)
| table cluster_name, pool_name, vendor, pool_size_bytes, pool_used_bytes, pool_used_pct, daily_growth_bytes, daily_growth_bytes_baseline, growth_burst_factor, eta_to_full_days, severity_bucket, freeze_active, business_service, tier, owner_email
```

Step 3 — Validate

Day-one checks: (1) `index=hci OR index=vmware OR index=nutanix sourcetype IN ("vmware:vsan:capacity","nutanix:storage_container") earliest=-1h@h | stats c by sourcetype` for each enabled platform. (2) Run the full saved search; `pool_used_pct` should be between 0 and 100 for well-formed pools. (3) `| eval rem=pool_size_bytes - pool_used_bytes | where rem < 0` should return no rows. (4) `| where isnotnull(eta_to_full_days) | table eta_to_full_days pool_name` and confirm plausibility when growth is non-zero. (5) `| inputlookup hci_pool_owner` row count matches governance. (6) Future `change_freeze_until` raises `freeze_active=1` and demotes sev for paging.

Step 4 — Operationalize

Panels: (1) gauge of `pool_used_pct` by `vendor` trellis; (2) `timechart` of `eta_to_full_days` for pools with finite ETA; (3) heat of `daily_growth_bytes` by `pool_name` and day; (4) top-10 by smallest positive `eta_to_full_days` with `isnotnull(eta_to_full_days)`; (5) result table. Drilldown passes `cluster_name`, `pool_name`, `vendor`. ITSI: one entity per pool. Tickets include `cluster_name`, `pool_name`, `vendor`, `pool_used_pct`, `eta_to_full_days`, `owner_email`, and a runbook link for pool expansion, Nutanix growth, vSAN capacity adds, or HyperFlex datastore work.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting

(1) vSAN: slack versus raw used can differ from a VMware UI if one view includes policy reserve. (2) Nutanix: `effective_used_size_bytes` versus `usage` on thin. (3) HyperFlex: `provisionedCapacityInBytes` is thin. (4) Azure Stack HCI: tiering and CSV maintenance. (5) `eta_to_full_days` null when no growth. (6) `streamstats` after restart needs 24h of samples; add a summary index for stable deltas. (7) HEC 400/403: single-line JSON, valid token, TLS. (8) `join` on `pool_name` typos. (9) `streamstats global=f` is correct per-pool; if distributed gaps appear, add `localop` pre-streamstats or summarize used bytes. (10) `daily_growth_bytes` still zero: poll interval longer than 24h window or index gap. (11) Inflated `growth_burst_factor`: `dgb_eff` at fallback 1. (12) Freeze parsing: try ISO date-only.
grandmaExplanation: We keep one check on the big storage pools the virtual machines share, and we show how full each is, about how long until it runs out if use keeps up, and who owns it. We also call out when space starts disappearing much faster than normal so the team can catch a runaway copy job before a weekend page.
hardware: Any supported HCI that exposes the four software-defined pool models above: vSAN on VMware HCL nodes; Nutanix on NX or partner appliances; Cisco HyperFlex HX; Azure Stack HCI with S2D and industry-standard NVMe, SAS, or hybrid tiers. The pool is the tenant-facing economic unit, not a single drive slot; physical sparing and disk-group UCs are separate. Media generation and all-flash versus hybrid change rebuild hours but the capacity math in Splunk stays bytes in versus bytes out of the same pool name.
